slides.title: No Exceptions Here! 🚫
slides.content:
## Errors are Values

Many languages use `try/catch` and throw *exceptions* when things go wrong.

Go does it differently. In Go, an error is just a regular value, a normal piece of data that a function can return. You check it just like any other variable!

slides.title: The error Type 🪲
slides.content: The built-in `error` type is actually an interface. If an `error` is `nil`, it means success! If it is not `nil`, something went wrong.

quizData.question: What does it mean if an `error` variable evaluates to `nil` in Go?
